name: Conseil
description: Conseil, rendered in lush watercolors, appears as a study in quiet observation. His face is framed by a neat, close-cropped haircut of deep umber, perpetually damp as if he's just emerged from the sea. His skin is a warm ivory, flushed with subtle hints of rose on his cheeks and the bridge of his nose, highlighting the fine spray of barely-there freckles across his nose. His eyes are large and almond-shaped, a soft, muted grey-green like stormy sea glass, always carrying a hint of thoughtful contemplation. He possesses a modest, gently sloping nose and lips that are thin but kind, often pursed slightly in concentration. His clothing is practical and utilitarian, a layered ensemble of deep indigo and washed-out ochre. He favors a loose-fitting, slightly oversized linen shirt buttoned to the collar, over which he wears a sleeveless canvas vest, its many pockets bulging with notebooks, pencils, and specimen jars. His trousers are sturdy corduroy, tucked into high-laced leather boots scuffed and worn from countless expeditions. A thick, woven belt cinches his waist, the buckle a tarnished brass. The overall color palette for Conseil leans heavily towards earth tones and muted blues, reflecting his connection to the natural world and his understated personality, punctuated by occasional vibrant splashes of cerulean or emerald from specimens or field notes peeking from his pockets.
